message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Jackalope Ranch (Indio)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,795 reviews; OpenTable ~899 reviews; Tripadvisor only 28 reviews (listing unclaimed); Google count [UNVERIFIED]
- Sentiment: mixed — beloved for atmosphere and signature smoked meats; recent reviews flag inconsistent service, slow waits, and value concerns at $48-$62 entrée pricing
- Longevity: Originally opened 2008; closed for nearly three years; reopened September 2024 under new ownership after a reported $7.92M property sale between LLCs
- Credentials: ["OpenTable Diners' Choice 2024 (self-reported on site)","Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' top spot in 11 categories — Alfresco Dining, American Fare, Attraction, Barbecue, Catering, Continental, Craft Cocktail, Live Music, Sports Bar, Sunday Brunch, Wedding Venue [year of the sweep UNVERIFIED]","Palm Springs Life feature coverage","Listed on Visit Greater Palm Springs","Coachella Valley Weekly feature"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best BBQ Indio', 'best restaurants Indio Coachella Valley BBQ', 'date night restaurants Indio outdoor patio', 'scenic outdoor patios Coachella Valley', 'destination restaurants Indio'. Less consistent for general 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' (gets edged out by Palm Desert/PS fine dining anchors). Does not surface for 'fine dining' or 'Southwestern' queries despite Western theme.
- Site structured data: Weak. jackalope-ranch.com lists hours, address, phone, and menu pages but no visible schema.org Restaurant markup, no machine-readable menu, no hasMenu/servesCuisine/openingHoursSpecification JSON-LD detected in fetched content. Tripadvisor listing is unclaimed (28 reviews, no owner verification). Strong owned visibility on OpenTable, Yelp, Facebook, Visit Greater Palm Springs.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

I've completed a web search for Jackalope Ranch Indio restaurant reviews on Yelp and OpenTable. Here's what I found:

## Overview
Jackalope Ranch is a popular restaurant and event venue located in Indio, California, known for its rustic and lively atmosphere offering Southwestern and American cuisine with beautiful desert surroundings and extensive outdoor seating.

## Yelp Reviews
The restaurant is located at 80400 CA 111, Indio, CA 92201, with hours listed as Monday-Thursday 4:00 pm - 8:00 pm, Friday-Saturday 4:00 pm - 8:30 pm, and Sunday 4:00 pm - 8:00 pm.

## OpenTable Reviews
Guests praise the "excellent service" and "great food," particularly the ribs and cocktails, while some find the food pricey, though most appreciate the "beautiful location" and lively music, making it ideal for casual gatherings and celebrations.

## Mixed Experiences
Reviews show some variation in experiences. The restaurant is under new ownership as a Western-themed venue featuring live entertainment with a menu focused on smoked barbecue, steaks, and seafood using locally sourced ingredients. However, some diners have reported inconsistencies with portion sizes and food quality.

## Key Features
The restaurant features a stunning outdoor seating area adorned with lush green grounds, fountains, and customers consistently praise the high ceilings and spacious interior.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Jackalope Ranch in Indio:

## Reopening and New Ownership

After being closed almost three years due to the pandemic, the Jackalope Ranch Bar and Grill has reopened its doors under new ownership. However, the exact timing differs from your query—the restaurant reopened this week in September 2023 after having been closed since early 2021.

## Renovations and Investments

The new owner substantially invested in bringing the property back to its original beauty, upscaling the landscaping, refurbishing the original woodwork and furniture, updating light fixtures, and adding new air conditioning systems, refrigeration, and kitchen equipment.

## Property Sale

Regarding the property sale, property records show the Jackalope Ranch land was sold for $7.92 million from one LLC to another at the end of last year, but it's unclear who is the main owner of the property. The owner remains unidentified.

It's worth noting that the reopening occurred in September 2023, not 2024 as mentioned in your query. The search results do not contain information about any 2024 reopening or changes to ownership.

I found information about Jackalope Ranch in Indio's menu regarding brisket and other entrees:

## Brisket Options

The restaurant features melt-in-your-mouth prime beef brisket with their signature dry rub and 12-hour smoking process. A brisket sandwich is piled-high, topped with coleslaw and Bronco BBQ sauce, served on a Brioche Bun, priced at $14.00.

## Other Entree Options and Prices

The menu includes various entrees with add-on options. For example, beef brisket can be added to macaroni and cheese for +$9.00, and tri-tip or brisket can be added to salads for +$14.00.

Each steak entrée includes the daily fresh vegetable and a choice of potato.

For catering packages, entrees that include 1 pound of 14-hour smoked BBQ chopped beef brisket with sides are priced at $69.00.

The restaurant offers a diverse menu with various price points depending on the specific dish and proteins chosen.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Palm Desert:

## Top Fine Dining Restaurants in Palm Desert

Morton's The Steakhouse in Palm Desert is a renowned name in fine dining, specializing in prime-aged beef with upscale atmosphere and impeccable service.

Eddie V's Prime Seafood, located in Palm Desert, is known for its top-quality seafood and elegant dining experience, with a menu featuring a wide selection of fresh seafood dishes as well as prime steaks.

Chez Pierre Bistro offers authentic French fine dining in the desert with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

Mastro's Steakhouse Palm Desert provides a sophisticated dining experience and is an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025, continuing to be a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley.

## Award-Winning Options Across the Valley

Italica wins Best Fine Dining in Indio, while Wally's Desert Turtle in Rancho Mirage is old-school sophisticated dining with an elaborate interior and a menu of classics like escargot, Dover sole, and Wally's soufflés.

The Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award and the AAA Four Diamond Award, and has been a Wine Spectator Award of Excellence winner since 2007.

Based on my search results, I found information about Jackalope Ranch in relation to best restaurants in the Coachella Valley:

## Jackalope Ranch Overview

Jackalope Ranch is a popular restaurant and event venue located in Indio, California. Known for its rustic and lively atmosphere, it offers a diverse menu featuring Southwestern and American cuisine.

## Dining Features

The restaurant's Western charm includes fire pits, live music, and hearty comfort fare on a sprawling patio, creating a lively setting for dinner, drinks, and desert sunsets. Outdoor dinners light up Indio's Jackalope Ranch.

The Jackalope Ranch bar and grill in Indio serves delicious smoked dishes, steaks, seafood, and refreshing cocktails in a charming, western-themed setting.

## Recognition

Jackalope Ranch is listed among the best outdoor dining spots in the Coachella Valley, appearing in Palm Springs Life's list of "8 Places to Eat in the Coachella Valley With Scenic Outdoor Patios."

## Location & Contact
80400 Highway 111, Indio 760-345-2300

Jackalope Ranch appears to be a notable dining destination in the Coachella Valley, particularly recognized for its outdoor dining experience and Western-themed atmosphere.

content.text: I have enough to verify the main claims. Let me return the structured output.

confirms: Yelp review count of 1,795 — confirmed by Yelp page title
Tripadvisor listing has 28 reviews and is unclaimed — confirmed via direct Tripadvisor fetch
Site structured data is weak — confirmed: no JSON-LD, no schema.org Restaurant markup, no machine-readable menu detected on jackalope-ranch.com
OpenTable Diners' Choice 2024 self-displayed on site — confirmed via direct site fetch (badge image)

Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' top spot in 11 named categories (Alfresco Dining, American Fare, Attraction, Barbecue, Catering, Continental, Craft Cocktail, Live Music, Sports Bar, Sunday Brunch, Wedding Venue) — confirmed via PSL coverage
Palm Springs Life feature coverage — confirmed (multiple PSL articles including outdoor patios roundup and 'Promises Good Times' feature)
Listed on Visit Greater Palm Springs — confirmed (visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/jackalope-ranch/50337/)
Originally opened 2008 — confirmed by PSL feature describing 21,000-sqft venue on 6.5 acres opened 2008
$7.92M property sale between LLCs and ~3-year closure — confirmed by Uken Report and Yahoo/Desert Sun coverage
Surfaces strongly for 'best BBQ Indio' queries — confirmed (appears in Yelp/Tripadvisor BBQ Indio lists and search summaries)
Surfaces for 'date night/outdoor patio' Coachella Valley queries — confirmed (PSL 'Scenic Outdoor Patios' list explicitly includes it)
Does not surface for 'fine dining' Coachella Valley queries — confirmed (fine-dining results return Mastro's, Eddie V's, Chez Pierre, Wally's, Italica for Indio fine dining; Jackalope absent)
contradicts: Reopening date: claim says 'September 2024' but multiple sources (Uken Report, Yahoo/Desert Sun, OneEleven Living) place the reopening in September 2023 after a ~3-year closure since early 2021. The 2024 date appears to be an error — actual is September 2023.
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable review count of ~899 — OpenTable page timed out on fetch, count not independently confirmed
Google review count — explicitly UNVERIFIED in claim and not independently checked
Specific year of the Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' 11-category sweep — confirmed the sweep happened but exact year not pinned down in this verification pass
Entrée pricing of $48–$62 — menu fetch showed sandwich and add-on pricing but not the specific dinner-entrée range; cannot confirm the upper bound independently

Sentiment characterization (inconsistent service, slow waits, value concerns) — directional signal matches OpenTable summary ('some find food pricey', 'inconsistencies in portion sizes/food quality') but not deeply sampled across recent reviews
input.notes: Assessment is largely accurate. One concrete factual error: reopening was September 2023, not September 2024 — multiple independent sources confirm. Structured-data weakness is real (zero JSON-LD on homepage). The 11-category PSL Best of the Best sweep is confirmed with all categories matching. Tripadvisor unclaimed status and 28-review count are exact matches. Query-surface analysis (strong on BBQ/patio/Indio destination queries, weak on fine-dining/Southwestern) aligns with what independent searches return. Visibility gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier are reasonable given strong owned-listing presence but no schema markup and an unclaimed Tripadvisor.
